prose:
Method–provenance summary:
Property: Atom-condensed Fukui functions (f⁺, f⁻, f0) and dual descriptor for benzaldehyde; headline value reported is the largest electrophilic Fukui index f⁺.
Level of theory: DFT, tier ‘standard’ → B3LYP/def2-TZVP with density fitting (PySCF backend).
Geometry provenance: fixed experimental geometry supplied by user (/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/benzaldehyde.xyz); single-point calculations for the neutral (N), cationic (N–1), and anionic (N+1) states were performed – a canonical DFT//exp-geom protocol (no further relaxation).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model; purely in vacuo).
Charge and multiplicity: neutral singlet (0, 1) for N state; doublet (+1, 2) and (–1, 2) for the N–1 and N+1 states used in the finite-difference Fukui evaluation.
Software: chemkit ‘fukui-reactivity’ skill (PySCF v2 backend reported). Live SCF output was streamed to its automatically generated .out file (path echoed at launch; e.g. fukui_<timestamp>.out) and the structured result saved to /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpfyzr80xm/live.json.
Convergence & caveats: All three SCFs (N, N±1) converged; integrity status ok, trustworthy = true. Warnings were issued at the “warning” level (see below) but all relevant charge-conservation checks passed. No other engine warnings.

Headline result:
Most electrophilic site: atom 3 (C, 0-based index 2) with f⁺ = 0.39938968 (dimensionless). This is the largest f⁺ value in the molecule and is therefore reported as the headline Fukui reactivity index.
Most nucleophilic site: atom 1 (O, 0-based index 0) with f⁻ = 0.23839906 (dimensionless) – supplied for completeness.

Verbatim warnings returned by the engine:
• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• common header present

All files needed for reproduction — the input geometry, result JSON, and PNG plot — are located under /home/erobles/chem-skills/… as shown in the CLI invocation above. The live engine log was written during execution to the standard chemkit out-log (path printed at job start and available for inspection with `tail -f`).
